page_title: "nestedFilter Function - utility-tools"
description: |-
  Filter a nested map by leaf object attributes
---

# `nestedFilter` Function

Filter a nested map by leaf object attributes

Collapses a nested map, filters the leaves by the given condition(s), then expands back to the original structure. Multiple condition objects are OR'd together; attributes within one object are AND'd. At least one condition is required.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
nestedFilter(input dynamic, condition dynamic...) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) The nested map or object to filter.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `condition` (Variadic, Dynamic, Nullable) One or more condition objects. A leaf is kept if it matches any one condition object (OR). Within each object all key=value pairs must match (AND).

## Example Usage

### Filter preserving nested structure

`nestedFilter` walks to the leaf objects and removes any that do not match the condition. Intermediate levels are pruned if they become empty.

```terraform
locals {
  services = {
    dev = {
      api = { port = 8080, tls = false }
      web = { port = 443,  tls = true  }
    }
    prod = {
      api = { port = 8080, tls = true }
      web = { port = 443,  tls = true }
    }
  }
}

output "tls_services" {
  value = provider::util::nestedFilter(local.services, { tls = true })
}
# Result:
# {
#   dev  = { web = { port = 443, tls = true } }
#   prod = { api = { port = 8080, tls = true }, web = { port = 443, tls = true } }
# }
```

### AND and OR conditions

Within a single condition object all attributes must match (AND). Passing multiple condition objects keeps leaves matching any of them (OR).

```terraform
locals {
  endpoints = {
    dev = {
      public  = { port = 443,  tls = true,  env = "dev" }
      private = { port = 8080, tls = false, env = "dev" }
    }
    prod = {
      public  = { port = 443,  tls = true,  env = "prod" }
      private = { port = 8080, tls = false, env = "prod" }
    }
  }
}

# AND: keep only TLS-enabled endpoints
output "tls_endpoints" {
  value = provider::util::nestedFilter(local.endpoints, { tls = true })
}
# Result: { dev = { public = {...} }, prod = { public = {...} } }

# OR: keep dev-public OR prod-private
output "mixed_selection" {
  value = provider::util::nestedFilter(local.endpoints, { tls = true, env = "dev" }, { tls = false, env = "prod" })
}
```

### Using with nestedCombine

A powerful pattern is to generate all combinations with `nestedCombine`, then select a meaningful subset with `nestedFilter`.

```terraform
locals {
  all = provider::util::nestedCombine([
    { key = "env",    items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region", items = ["europe-west1", "us-central1"] },
    { key = "tier",   items = {
      api    = { replicas = 2, tls = false }
      web    = { replicas = 3, tls = true  }
      worker = { replicas = 1, tls = false }
    }},
  ])
}

# Keep only TLS-enabled tiers across all environments and regions.
output "tls_tiers" {
  value = provider::util::nestedFilter(local.all, { tls = true })
}
# Result:
# {
#   dev = {
#     europe-west1 = { web = { id = "web", replicas = 3, tls = true } }
#     us-central1  = { web = { id = "web", replicas = 3, tls = true } }
#   }
#   prod = {
#     europe-west1 = { web = { id = "web", replicas = 3, tls = true } }
#     us-central1  = { web = { id = "web", replicas = 3, tls = true } }
#   }
# }
```
